description_long: This is a rectangular adhesive bandage, commonly known as a band-aid, with a light beige color and a central absorbent pad. The main body of the bandage features a grid of small, evenly spaced white dots, likely representing perforations or a breathable texture. One side of the bandage displays a slightly raised, rectangular pad, while the opposite side shows a flat, white absorbent area. The bandage is thin and flexible, designed to adhere to the skin and cover minor wounds or cuts. The surface appears matte with a soft, semi-flexible texture typical of medical adhesive products.
description: A beige rectangular adhesive bandage with a central white pad and a grid of small white dots, designed for covering minor wounds.
description_view_0: Front view showing the beige bandage with a raised central pad and a grid of white dots.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the bandage and the slight elevation of the central pad.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat white absorbent pad and the perforated beige backing.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the slim profile and central pad thickness.
attribute_packaging_design: No external packaging is visible; the bandage itself is exposed, showing a rectangular shape with a beige color and white pad.
attribute_adhesive_strip_pattern: The adhesive strip area features a regular grid of small white dots, likely representing perforations for breathability.
attribute_perforation_marks: Small, evenly spaced white dots on the beige area, likely indicating perforations for ventilation.
